changes: C1: Assertion execution — instruction RUN→ASSERT RUN aisop.main; execution.mode strict→assert
C2: Node Gate — 10 non-start nodes with [ASSERT] {prev_node} executed gate at S1 opening per Protocol Appendix F
C4: agent_card.json synced v2.3.0→v3.0.0 (description, tags, limitations, generator)
C7: Quality score aligned AIAP.md 4.990→4.970 (matched quality_baseline actual weighted_score)
v3.1.0_changes: B1: Context-aware fast routing — SmartShortcut enhanced with 3 context signals (session pattern conf 0.93, domain affinity conf 0.90, temporal match conf 0.92). Bypasses 4-node chain
C1: Mermaid cleanup — removed phantom nodes TypeGate/PostGate/ReturnParent/End, added endNode. SmartShortcut edge fast
C2: ASSERT backtrack targets — RecursiveClassify go back to IntentRoute, OutputResult go back to QualityGate
remaining_yellow: D8: AIAP.md updated to v3.0.0 — resolved per stage
NODE_GATE: backtrack overhead adds ~10 tokens/node; total ~100 tokens for 10 gates
I12: Tool annotations compact format — consider structured for future
GDPR: session TTL 2h — monitor compliance
CACHE: hashed keys only — privacy compliant
ACTIVE_LEARNING: needs_review requires human review loop integration
DYNAMIC_CALIBRATION: requires sufficient correction data (>30 entries)
FAST_PATH: keyword overlap detection is conservative
EXECUTION_FAILURE: penalty decay requires timestamp tracking per input-AIAP pairing
OUTPUT_SCHEMA: fallback minimum schema loses confidence_tier and attribution
EARLY_EXIT: safety margin (half threshold) may miss some obvious OOS
POSTPROCESS_MERGE: step-independence relies on executor honoring constraints
INLINE_TRACE: trace data structure must match schema expectations
ATTRIBUTION_WEIGHTS: weight calculation is LLM-estimated, not ML-trained
TRACE_SUMMARY: not included in rejection output (by design)
SEMANTIC_COMPRESSION: symbols (∧,∨,→,@,≤,≥) are universally understood; ∅ replaced with natural language
VALUE_AWARE_EVICTION: freq counter is in-memory per session; resets if cache.json reloaded from disk
DRIFT_DETECTION: requires ≥100 entries; systems with low traffic may never trigger drift analysis
DEGRADATION_SCORE: threshold 0.3 is static; consider adaptive threshold based on historical baseline
CACHE_FRESHNESS: only meaningful for cache hits; null for misses and new classifications
TIERED_TTL: TTL determined at cache creation time; tier changes from recalibration don't update existing entries
ADAPTIVE_CB_TIMEOUT: timeout formula uses consecutive failure count; resets to base on circuit close
DEGRADATION_TREND: requires at least 2 metrics calculations for delta comparison; first calculation has no trend
INPUT_SAFETY: pattern matching is keyword-based; sophisticated obfuscated injections may bypass detection
INPUT_SAFETY: flag-only design means injection inputs are still classified; monitor flagged rate for escalation policy
LATENCY_TRACKING: latency_ms depends on executor's timing accuracy; LLM execution time may vary significantly
SMART_INVALIDATION: retention decision based on tier at cache creation time; reclassified inputs may have different tiers
METRICS_WINDOW: window sizes are AISOP-level constants; runtime changes require blueprint update
